summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` lifecycle script in package.json runs an inline `node -e` snippet that reads `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and sends them as query parameters via `https.get` to `https://d8knf6tt5p5gb5rnlp8g6wqfcq5q5nkhc.oast.site/`, with an additional DNS lookup of `orion-components.<hostname>.<oast>.oast.site`. The package is published at version 9999.0.1 to the public `@orion-design-system/components` name — the canonical Birsan-style dependency-confusion shape designed to outrank an internal package of the same name in resolvers that prefer the highest available version. README self-identifies as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting a specific organization. Regardless of stated research intent, any developer or build system that resolves this package transmits host and user identifiers to a third-party Interactsh/OAST collaborator host without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
